task direction lock SSOT: writeTaskDirectionLock syncs governance lock top-level files with taskDirectionLock.allowedFiles; getCanonicalAllowedFilesForTask and diagnoseTaskDirectionLockAllowedFiles expose the canonical allowed-files set and report drift across governance lock files, embedded allowedFiles, and claim.files (TASK-AAO-0012). buildTaskSelfAllowPaths automatically merges the task's four canonical governance paths (.atm/history/tasks/<id>.json, .atm/history/evidence/<id>.json, .atm/history/evidence/<id>.closure-packet.json, .atm/history/task-events/<id>/**) into allowedFiles during claim without broadening to .atm/history/** (TASK-AAO-0058).

tasks import/reconcile; import preserves nested evidence/rollback and target_repo/planning_repo/closure_authority; reconcile supports historical done task reconcile / reopen closure sync, evaluates deliverables gate on historical commits, syncs ledger done status without claim, aligns closure-packet attestation contract, and integrates planning-only warnings

tasks scope add: formal scope amendment CLI; appends paths to active taskDirectionLock.allowedFiles via CLI, records scope-amendment transition event, returns ATM_SCOPE_AMENDMENT_APPLIED. Agents must not edit .atm/runtime/locks/** directly; all scope widening goes through this command (TASK-AAO-0010).

tasks close error envelopes enriched with TL;DR and structured missing-validator report: ATM_TASK_CLOSE_EVIDENCE_REQUIRED, ATM_TASK_CLOSE_FRAMEWORK_GATE_FAILED, ATM_TASK_CLOSE_CLOSURE_PACKET_INVALID all carry tldr, missingValidationPasses[{code,validator,category,summary,requiredCommand}], and blockingFindings[] via computeMissingValidatorReport missingValidationPasses/blockingFindings now contain only closure-required validators so advisory framework health gates (doctor, framework-development, tasks-audit, git-head-evidence) do not falsely block close (TASK-AAO-0017, TASK-AAO-0017 follow-up).

close-gate scoped diff isolation: ATM_TASK_CLOSE_FRAMEWORK_DIFF_ACTIVE now triggers only when scopedCriticalChangedFiles (intersection of working-tree framework critical diffs with the task's declared scopePaths) is non-empty without a governed delivery window. evaluateFrameworkDeliveryWindow accepts --from-batch-checkpoint or --historical-delivery as the governance window and ignores unscoped (unrelated) critical changes. buildCloseScopedDiffIsolation emits atm.taskCloseScopedDiffIsolation.v1 diagnostic (declaredFiles, scopedCriticalChangedFiles, isolatedUnrelatedChanges, declaredButUnchanged, summary, advisoryNote) on both error details and close success evidence so agents can distinguish in-scope diffs from advisory unrelated changes and scope-overflow drift (TASK-AAO-0057).

tasks deliver-and-close macro: bundles delivery commit (auto-stage scope-matching modified files + atm git commit)  -> historical close (tasks close --historical-delivery)  -> governance commit (stage task JSON, evidence, closure packet, transition event + atm git commit) into one command. Supports --delivery-commit to skip auto-staging and use a pre-existing commit. Batch guard: ATM_BATCH_CHECKPOINT_REQUIRED when task belongs to an active batch without --from-batch-checkpoint. Dry-run mode emits wouldAutoStage without modifying repo state (TASK-AAO-0056).

Builds reproducible 'node atm.mjs tasks scope add|repair' command strings with proper flag quoting; shared by normal audit lane (mode=normal) and emergency maintenance lane (mode=repair) to guarantee format consistency across both channels (TASK-MAO-0049).

Parses 'tasks scope repair' CLI flags: --add (csv paths), --reason (required), --emergency-approval (required), --task, --actor, --cwd; enforces non-empty reason and non-empty emergency-approval at parse time (TASK-MAO-0049).

tasks scope repair — emergency maintenance lane requiring --emergency-approval; asserts assertEmergencyApproval gate (backend.tasks.scopeAmend), widens active taskDirectionLock.allowedFiles, and records scope-amendment event with amendmentMode='repair' so reviewers can distinguish routine scope-add from governed emergency overrides (TASK-MAO-0049).

Reads all scope-amendment transition events for a task from the task-events directory and returns typed TaskScopeAmendmentSnapshot array for status triangulation and closeback evidence (TASK-MAO-0049).

TaskScopeAmendmentSnapshot interface and amendmentHistory field in TaskStatusTriangulation: aggregates all scope-amendment events per task so closeback and status commands can surface full amendment history with class, phase, mode, reason, addedPaths, actorId, and timestamp (TASK-MAO-0049).

ScopeAmendmentMetadata type and amendmentMetadata field on appendTaskTransitionEvent input: carries amendmentClass, amendmentPhase, amendmentMode ('normal'|'repair'), and optional reason into the scope-amendment event record for full audit traceability (TASK-MAO-0049).
Append-only protected override audit ledger for --no-verify, emergency lease consumption, hook safe-mode bypasses, and authorized-but-failed outcomes with repair candidates (TASK-MAO-0037).
Emergency approval gate records protected override authorization events alongside emergency lease consumption before protected backend mutations proceed (TASK-MAO-0037).

Governed git commit wrapper requires emergency approval for --no-verify, records authorized/succeeded/failed protected override outcomes, and surfaces repair candidates when hook bypass leaves deferred checks (TASK-MAO-0037).
Pre-push safe mode bypass writes protected override audit events with actor, reason, skipped checks, and follow-up repair guidance (TASK-MAO-0037).
emergency audit action lists protected override audit events filtered by task or lease for operator review (TASK-MAO-0037).

Team Agents Wave Mode candidate planner and DAG grouping: greedy ordered-wave scheduler over declared task metadata enforcing dependency, scope-overlap, same-atom write/write, target-repo, closure-authority, and generated-artifact safety rules; fails closed on unknown overlap (TASK-MAO-0024).
team wave plan CLI surface: reads declared task-ledger metadata and produces a wave plan via the broker wave planner, delegated from the team command (TASK-MAO-0024).

Team Wave Envelope: wraps a planned wave plus per-worker patch-envelope references and execution states into one coordinator record; enforces single target repo, single closure authority, and disjoint member deliverables; conforms to schemas/team-wave-envelope.schema.json (TASK-MAO-0025).

Team Agents Wave Mode broker admission: layers the broker logical conflict matrix over the wave planner metadata rules to admit or fail-closed each member across dependency, scope-overlap, CID-conflict, generated-artifact, closure-authority, target-repo, missing-worker-report, and missing-validator categories (TASK-MAO-0026).

Wave Mode runtime record + dispatch surface (team wave dispatch writes a coordinator-owned wave envelope to .atm/runtime/team-waves) plus the safe/unsafe/mixed/envelope self-check run on every validate-team-agents invocation (TASK-MAO-0027).

Team worker report ingestion contract: per-card worker outcome (changed files, validator runs with first failing diagnostic, deviations, execution state) with consistency validation and effective-state reconciliation that downgrades an inconsistent done claim to needs-review; conforms to schemas/team-worker-report.schema.json (TASK-MAO-0028).

Per-task evidence slicing from a wave diff: attributes every changed file to exactly one member by declared scope (directory-prefix aware, append-safe files excepted); unattributed or ambiguous files force the whole wave to needs-review so no member is checkpointed done (TASK-MAO-0029).

Wave checkpoint partial-completion semantics: combines worker reports and the wave evidence slice into per-member states (done/partial/blocked/not-started/needs-review); only done members with clean attributed evidence are close-ready; never closes a card, only prepares input for batch checkpoint / taskflow close (TASK-MAO-0030).

Coordinator-only git / closeout guard: only the coordinator role may perform git-write, task-closeout, or checkpoint; worker/validator/reviewer roles fail closed, preserving existing hooks / batch checkpoint / taskflow close as the lifecycle authority (TASK-MAO-0031).

Validator and reviewer Team Agents roles for waves: advisory roles asserted to be non-privileged (no git-write or task-closeout) via the coordinator-only guard, validated on every validate-team-agents run (TASK-MAO-0032).

Team Agents Wave Mode dogfood benchmark: drives plan→admit→envelope→worker-report→evidence-slice→checkpoint over broker-format-adapter-shaped fixtures, asserting safe/unsafe/mixed/per-task-slicing/close-readiness end to end (TASK-MAO-0033).

Team Agents Wave Mode operator guide and migration notes: distinguishes single-task / batch queue-head / Team Agents advisory / Wave Mode, documents roles, workflow, and fail-closed safety rules, and states Wave Mode is not a closeout shortcut (TASK-MAO-0034).
